steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The claim states Mrs. Sommers makes decisions "without conscious deliberation" — she acts on impulse, not reflection. The correct quotation must show her acting without thinking.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘"She handed the girl a five-dollar bill and waited for her change and for her parcel. What a very small parcel it was!"’: Shows her reacting to what she observes — not acting without thought. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘"She had no time—no second of time to devote to the past. The needs of the present absorbed her every faculty."’: Shows her absorbed by present needs, but it does not directly show a specific decision made without conscious deliberation. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘"She was not thinking at all. She seemed for the time to be taking a rest from that laborious and fatiguing function and to have abandoned herself to some mechanical impulse that directed her actions and freed her of responsibility."’: Explicitly states she wasn't thinking, and describes surrender to "some mechanical impulse that directed her actions." Directly supports the claim. ✓
  • Choice D — ‘"Mrs. Sommers selected a black pair and looked at them very long and closely. She pretended to be examining their texture, which the clerk assured her was excellent."’: Shows her observing and responding to surroundings — not unconscious action. ✗
That confirms C is correct.
